rulings.Case Title: EUGENE PITT SHEFFIELD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of E. Mitchell, Inc.S Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel production of E. Mitchell, Inc.¿s custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and request for production of documents is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Or Its Attorney
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion for monetary sanctions against defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and/or its attorney is granted. Sanctions of $2040 are awarded and shall be paid by defendant¿s counsel within 15 days of the date of hearing on this motion. The court used its discretion to refuse to consider defendant¿s late-filed and late-served opposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiffs Interrogatories From Mission Stucco Company
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 10, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Brayton Purcell plaintiff¿s motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiffs interrogatories from defendant Mission Stucco Co. is granted. Defendant has 5 days to provide verified responses. Plaintiff¿s request that the hearing on any dispositive motions filed by defendant in asbestos cases before this court be stayed until 60 days after defendant has provided verified responses is denied. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions of $1061 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
